title: Risky Lip Techniques
excerpt: I've had one occlusion in 12 years now so my rates are really good and that's primarily due to getting the depth correct top of the list has to be most common place to cause an occlusion probably due to the frequency of procedures which is lip augmentation you need to be aware of…

segments.text: i've had one occlusion in 12 years now so my rates are really good and that's primarily due to getting the depth correct top of the list has to be most common place to cause an occlusion probably due to the frequency of procedures which is lip augmentation you need to be aware of your injections of superior and inferior labial arteries both run parallel with the lips many injection techniques teach you to inject in a way that's parallel with the vermilion border what kind of injection techniques so the classic you know vermilion border philtrum injections anywhere where you're running the needle along the vermilion border you're likely to be close to that artery so it's probably the most common way to augment lips is to inject that way so one of the pros of these some of the tenting or russian lip techniques where you're running at 90 degrees at least you're not parallel with a vessel and although many will say that bruising is much worse um you're less likely to actually fill a blood vessel with dermal filler so that's the kind of thing you might do to make it a little bit safer

little bit safer you might choose a different technique and inject smaller amounts at a time being aware of your depth is really critical so i've had one inclusion in 12 years now so my rates are really good and that's primarily due to getting the depth correct so not injecting too deep knowing that the artery tends to run just beneath the muscle in most people unfortunately not in everyone but you're you've got that depth to protect you i know that new injectors often you can tell their resolution is not that high so that it looks to the untrained eye that there is similar depth but someone who's been injecting for five ten years will know immediately that's a bit too deep so we need to be careful of depth in particular when injecting lips

transcript: i've had one occlusion in 12 years now so my rates are really good and that's primarily due to getting the depth correct top of the list has to be most common place to cause an occlusion probably due to the frequency of procedures which is lip augmentation you need to be aware of your injections of superior and inferior labial arteries both run parallel with the lips many injection techniques teach you to inject in a way that's parallel with the vermilion border what kind of injection techniques so the classic you know vermilion border philtrum injections anywhere where you're running the needle along the vermilion border you're likely to be close to that artery so it's probably the most common way to augment lips is to inject that way so one of the pros of these some of the tenting or russian lip techniques where you're running at 90 degrees at least you're not parallel with a vessel and although many will say that bruising is much worse um you're less likely to actually fill a blood vessel with dermal filler so that's the kind of thing you might do to make it a little bit safer little bit safer you might choose a different technique and inject smaller amounts at a time being aware of your depth is really critical so i've had one inclusion in 12 years now so my rates are really good and that's primarily due to getting the depth correct so not injecting too deep knowing that the artery tends to run just beneath the muscle in most people unfortunately not in everyone but you're you've got that depth to protect you i know that new injectors often you can tell their resolution is not that high so that it looks to the untrained eye that there is similar depth but someone who's been injecting for five ten years will know immediately that's a bit too deep so we need to be careful of depth in particular when injecting lips
